notes: Core IMI product; three-tier client-server order fulfillment; still active as IMI Supply Chain platform
Database abstraction layer enabling Oracle, Sybase independence; succeeded by modern ORM frameworks
IMI chose TRIMtools for platform independence; Trifox products discontinued
IMI's Internet commerce layer; read-only browser access in 1997; evolved into full B2B e-commerce
Oracle 7 as System ESS database; Oracle 8i (1997) and beyond succeeded it
IMI's differentiating concept; demand-driven supply chain became industry standard (S&OP, demand sensing)
System ESS EDI Workbench for trading partner integration; EDI still used but supplemented by APIs
IMI Phase I roadmap included ORB and Java applets; browser Java applets became obsolete by ~2010
IMI's differentiating promotion management capability for CPG; remains core competency
System ESS presentation/application/database separation; conceptually evolved into n-tier/microservices
